description: Adds velocity tracking to the board stats panel — shows issues completed in the last N days with a 7d/30d window selector. The feature is fully functional and all tests pass. All findings require identifying violations of SOLID design principles in the new code.

findings.description: VelocityStats includes lastCompletedFormatted: string, which violates the Single Responsibility Principle. computeVelocityStats is a domain computation function in a lib module; its sole responsibility is to derive velocity metrics from raw issue data. Producing a formatted display string (e.g. 'Jun 3') is a presentation concern that belongs in the component consuming the result. Embedding it here means the function must change for two unrelated reasons: (1) a change to the velocity algorithm, and (2) a change to how the date is displayed (locale, format, surrounding text). The fix is to remove lastCompletedFormatted from VelocityStats and derive the display string inside BoardStats.tsx from the raw lastDate.

The velocity window selector renders two hard-coded <button> elements — one for '7d' and one for '30d' — with duplicated class logic. This violates the Open/Closed Principle: adding a third window (e.g. '90d') requires modifying the component's JSX by adding a third button block, rather than extending a data structure. The fix is to define a VELOCITY_WINDOWS constant (e.g. ['7d', '30d'] as VelocityWindow[]) and render the buttons by mapping over it, so adding a new option only requires extending the array.
